name: Basic Block Processing
pages: Let $(item)anvil$() fall on the block to process the block. The flowing pages of this entry will introduce relevant content in sequence: $(br)$(l:basic/block_processing#crush)Crush$()$(br)$(l:basic/block_processing#press)Press$()$(br)$(l:basic/block_processing#daub)Daub$()$(br)$(l:basic/block_processing#squeeze)Squeeze$()$(br)$(l:basic/block_processing#break)Break$()$(br)Before that, understanding how magnets help anvil machining will make it easier for you to get started.

pages.name: Magnets Control Anvils
pages.text: The magnet is demagnetized when activated by a redstone signal, and the anvil is dropped to perform the operation.

pages.name: Crush
pages.text: The list on the right gives more examples of where such transformation can occur, some of which can occur consecutively.

pages: $(item)Cobblestone$() ŌåÆ $(item)Gravel$() ŌåÆ $(item)Sand$();$(br)$(item)Polished Granite$() ŌåÆ $(item)Granite$() ŌåÆ $(item)Red Sand$();$(br)$(item)Polished Andesite$() ŌåÆ $(item)Andesite$() ŌåÆ $(item)Cinerite$();$(br)$(item)Polished Diorite$() ŌåÆ $(item)Diorite$() ŌåÆ $(item)Quartz Sand$();$(br)$(thing)Blocks with cracked variants$() ŌåÆ $(thing)Their cracked variants$();$(br)$(item)Netherrack$() ŌåÆ $(item)Nether Dust$();$(br)$(item)End Stone$() ŌåÆ $(item)End Dust$();$(br)$(item)Soul Soil$() ŌåÆ $(item)Soul Sand$();

pages.name: Press
pages.text: The list on the right gives more examples of where such transformation can occur.

pages: $(item)Moss Block$() + $(item)Dirt$() ŌåÆ $(item)Grass Block$();$(br)$(thing)Any Leaves$() + $(item)Dirt$() ŌåÆ $(item)Podzol$();$(br)$(thing)Any Mushroom Block$() + $(item)Dirt$() ŌåÆ $(item)Mycelium$();$(br)$(item)Nether Wart Block$() + $(item)Netherrack$() ŌåÆ $(item)Crimson Nylium$();$(br)$(item)Warped Wart Block$() + $(item)Netherrack$() ŌåÆ $(item)Warped Nylium$();$(br)$(item)Stone$() + $(item)Stone$() ŌåÆ $(item)Deepslate$();$(br)$(item)Ice$() + $(item)Ice$() ŌåÆ $(item)Packed Ice$();$(br)$(item)Packed Ice$() + $(item)Packed Ice$() ŌåÆ $(item)Blue Ice$();$(br)$(item)Basalt$() + $(item)Basalt$() ŌåÆ $(item)Blackstone$()

pages.name: Daub
pages.text: Unlike pressing, the block above will not be consumed. The list on the right gives more examples of where such transformation can occur.

pages: $(item)Moss Block$() + $(item)Cobblestone$() ŌåÆ $(item)Mossy Stone$();$(br)$(item)Moss Block$() + $(item)Stone Bricks$() ŌåÆ $(item)Moss Stone Bricks$();$(br)$(item)Honeycomb Block$() + $(thing)Any Copper Block$() ŌåÆ $(thing)Their Waxed variants$()

pages.name: Squeeze
pages.text: A single layer of liquid will appear in the cauldron. The list on the right gives more examples of where such transformation can occur, some of which can occur consecutively.

pages: $(item)Wet Sponge$() ŌåÆ $(item)Sponge$() + $(item)Water$();$(br)$(item)Moss blocks$() ŌåÆ $(item)Moss Carpet$() + $(item)Water$();$(br)$(item)Magma Block$() ŌåÆ $(item)Netherrack$() + $(item)Lava$();$(br)$(item)Snow Block$() ŌåÆ $(item)Ice$() + $(item)Powder Snow$();$(br)$(item)Full Bee Nest$() ŌåÆ $(item)Empty Bee Nest$() + $(item)Honey$()(Specially, when there are three layers of $(item)Honey$() below, if you continue to squeeze the $(item)Full Bee Nest$(), it will be crushed into a $(item)Honey Block$(). The above $(item)Bee Nest$() can be replaced with $(item)Beehive$())

pages.name: break
pages.text: Block becomes dropped item. Detailed instructions on the right.
